Core frameworks for building web applications and APIs

column3: Microsoft's cross-platform web framework. The foundation for all .NET web apps - includes MVC, Razor Pages, Web API, Minimal APIs, and SignalR for real-time. Built-in DI, middleware pipeline, and extensive ecosystem.
column4: Excellent - Microsoft-backed, extremely stable, enterprise-grade, massive community
column5: Essential - This is your primary web framework. Use Minimal APIs for microservices and Controllers for complex enterprise APIs.

column3: Thin layer over ASP.NET Core Minimal APIs providing a more structured, modular approach with automatic route discovery and validation integration.
column4: Very Good - Stable, well-maintained, clean API design
column5: Recommended for teams preferring modular endpoint organization over controllers

column3: High-performance alternative to MVC controllers following REPR pattern (Request-Endpoint-Response). Built-in validation, auth, and OpenAPI support with minimal boilerplate.
column4: Excellent - Very active development, great performance, growing adoption
column5: Highly Recommended - Modern approach combining best of Minimal APIs and structured endpoints

column3: Microsoft's official ORM. Supports LINQ, migrations, change tracking, lazy loading, complex relationships. Excellent PostgreSQL support via Npgsql provider.

column3: Micro-ORM focused on raw SQL performance. Simple object mapping without change tracking overhead. Created by Stack Overflow team.

column5: Highly Recommended - Use alongside EF Core for performance-critical queries and reporting

column3: Extends EF Core with bulk operations (BulkInsert, BulkUpdate, BulkDelete, BulkMerge). Massive performance improvement for batch operations.

column3: Document database and event store using PostgreSQL's JSON capabilities. Combines NoSQL flexibility with PostgreSQL reliability.
